question: What is Grouped Query Attention (GQA)? How does it differ from MHA and MQA?
code_hint: GQA: n_heads_kv < n_heads_q, shares K/V across groups, fewer KV heads
expected_output: MHA: n_kv = n_q. MQA: n_kv = 1. GQA: n_kv < n_q (e.g., 4 or 8 groups)
template:
# MHA: Multi-Head Attention - each head has separate K,V (n_kv = n_q)
# MQA: Multi-Query Attention - all heads share single K,V (n_kv = 1)
# GQA: Grouped Query Attention - n_kv < n_q, K/V shared across groups

print("MHA: n_kv = n_q. MQA: n_kv = 1. GQA: n_kv < n_q (e.g., 4 or 8 groups)")
answer:
# MHA: Multi-Head Attention - each head has separate K,V (n_kv = n_q)
# MQA: Multi-Query Attention - all heads share single K,V (n_kv = 1)
# GQA: Grouped Query Attention - n_kv < n_q, K/V shared across groups

print("MHA: n_kv = n_q. MQA: n_kv = 1. GQA: n_kv < n_q (e.g., 4 or 8 groups)")
